judgmentZh: 萃：亨。王假有庙，利见大人，亨，利贞。用大牲吉，利有攸往。
judgmentEn: Gathering Together. Success. The king approaches his temple. It furthers one to see the great man. This brings success. Perseverance furthers. To bring great offerings creates good fortune. It furthers one to undertake something.
imageZh: 泽上于地，萃；君子以除戎器，戒不虞。
imageEn: Over the earth, the lake: the image of Gathering Together. Thus the superior man renews his weapons in order to meet the unforeseen.
interpretationZh: 萃卦象征聚集，泽水汇聚于地上。人心聚合，宜以诚敬之心祭祀，团结众人共成大事。
interpretationEn: Cui symbolizes gathering and assembly. The lake collects over the earth. Unite people with sincerity and reverence to accomplish great things.

lines.textZh: 初六：有孚不终，乃乱乃萃，若号，一握为笑，勿恤，往无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: If you are sincere, but not to the end, there will sometimes be confusion, sometimes gathering. If you call out, then after one grasp of the hand you can laugh again. Regret not. Going is without blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 诚信不能坚持到底，时聚时散。呼唤同伴，握手言欢，前往无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: Sincerity wavers, causing confusion. Call out for help, join hands, and move forward without blame.

lines.textZh: 六二：引吉，无咎，孚乃利用禴。
lines.textEn: Six in the second place: Letting oneself be drawn brings good fortune and remains blameless. If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering.
lines.interpretationZh: 被引导而聚合则吉，无咎。心怀诚信，即使简单的祭祀也有利。
lines.interpretationEn: Being drawn into the gathering brings fortune. With sincerity, even a small offering furthers.

lines.textZh: 六三：萃如嗟如，无攸利，往无咎，小吝。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: Gathering together amid sighs. Nothing that would further. Going is without blame. Slight humiliation.
lines.interpretationZh: 聚合中叹息不已，无所利益。前往虽无咎，但有小憾。
lines.interpretationEn: Gathering with sighs. No advantage here. Going forward is blameless but slightly humiliating.

lines.textZh: 九四：大吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: Great good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 大吉大利，没有过失。以公心聚众，自然顺遂。
lines.interpretationEn: Great good fortune and no blame. Gathering others with a public spirit brings success.

lines.textZh: 九五：萃有位，无咎。匪孚，元永贞，悔亡。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: If in gathering together one has position, this is without blame. If there are some who are not yet sincerely in the work, sublime and enduring perseverance is needed. Then remorse disappears.
lines.interpretationZh: 居位而聚众，无咎。若有人尚未诚服，需以恒久正道感化，悔恨自消。
lines.interpretationEn: Gathering from a position of authority, no blame. Win over doubters through enduring perseverance and remorse vanishes.

lines.textZh: 上六：赍咨涕洟，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Lamenting and sighing, floods of tears. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 叹息流泪，悲伤不已，但并无过失。真情流露，终能感动人心。
lines.interpretationEn: Lamenting with floods of tears. No blame. Genuine emotion ultimately moves others.

judgmentZh: 升：元亨，用见大人，勿恤，南征吉。
judgmentEn: Pushing Upward has supreme success. One must see the great man. Fear not. Departure toward the south brings good fortune.
imageZh: 地中生木，升；君子以顺德，积小以高大。
imageEn: Within the earth, wood grows: the image of Pushing Upward. Thus the superior man of devoted character heaps up small things in order to achieve something high and great.
interpretationZh: 升卦象征上升，木在地中生长向上。顺应自然之德，积少成多，由小而大，循序渐进。
interpretationEn: Sheng symbolizes upward growth. Wood grows within the earth, rising steadily. Accumulate small gains to achieve greatness through gradual progress.

lines.textZh: 初六：允升，大吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: Pushing upward that meets with confidence brings great good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 诚信上升，大吉大利。初始阶段得到信任，顺利上进。
lines.interpretationEn: Ascending with trust and confidence. Great good fortune at the outset.

lines.textZh: 九二：孚乃利用禴，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: If one is sincere, it furthers one to bring even a small offering. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 心怀诚信，即使简单的祭祀也有利，无咎。
lines.interpretationEn: With sincerity, even a small offering furthers. No blame.

lines.textZh: 九三：升虚邑。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: One pushes upward into an empty city.
lines.interpretationZh: 上升进入空虚之城，畅通无阻，毫无障碍。
lines.interpretationEn: Pushing upward into an empty city. No obstacles on the path.

lines.textZh: 六四：王用亨于岐山，吉，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The king offers him Mount Ch'i. Good fortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 君王在岐山祭祀，吉利无咎。顺应天命，恭敬奉献。
lines.interpretationEn: The king makes offerings at Mount Ch'i. Good fortune and no blame through devoted service.

lines.textZh: 六五：贞吉，升阶。
lines.textEn: Six in the fifth place: Perseverance brings good fortune. One pushes upward by steps.
lines.interpretationZh: 坚守正道则吉，一步一步稳步上升。
lines.interpretationEn: Perseverance brings fortune. Ascend step by step in steady progress.

lines.textZh: 上六：冥升，利于不息之贞。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: Pushing upward in darkness. It furthers one to be unremittingly persevering.
lines.interpretationZh: 在昏暗中上升，利于不懈地坚守正道。盲目上进需以恒心自持。
lines.interpretationEn: Ascending in darkness. Unceasing perseverance is needed. Blind ambition requires steadfast discipline.

judgmentZh: 困：亨，贞，大人吉，无咎，有言不信。
judgmentEn: Oppression. Success. Perseverance. The great man brings about good fortune. No blame. When one has something to say, it is not believed.
imageZh: 泽无水，困；君子以致命遂志。
imageEn: There is no water in the lake: the image of Exhaustion. Thus the superior man stakes his life on following his will.
interpretationZh: 困卦象征困穷，泽中无水，枯竭之象。君子身处困境，宁可舍命也要实现志向，言语不被信任时以行动证明。
interpretationEn: Kun symbolizes oppression and exhaustion. The lake is without water. The superior man stakes his life on his convictions, proving himself through action when words are not believed.

lines.textZh: 初六：臀困于株木，入于幽谷，三岁不觌。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: One sits oppressed under a bare tree and strays into a gloomy valley. For three years one sees nothing.
lines.interpretationZh: 困坐于枯木之下，迷入幽暗山谷，三年不见光明。处境极为困顿。
lines.interpretationEn: Sitting oppressed under a bare tree, lost in a dark valley. For three years, no light. A time of deep hardship.

lines.textZh: 九二：困于酒食，朱绂方来，利用享祀，征凶，无咎。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: One is oppressed while at meat and drink. The man with the scarlet knee bands is just coming. It furthers one to offer sacrifice. To set forth brings misfortune. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 在酒食中受困，贵人即将到来。宜祭祀祈福，出征则凶，但无过失。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed amid plenty. A dignitary approaches. Offer sacrifice; do not march forth. No blame.

lines.textZh: 六三：困于石，据于蒺藜，入于其宫，不见其妻，凶。
lines.textEn: Six in the third place: One lets himself be oppressed by stone, and leans on thorns and thistles. He enters his house and does not see his wife. Misfortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 被巨石所困，倚靠荆棘，回到家中却不见妻子，凶险。进退失据，内外皆困。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed by stone, leaning on thorns. Entering home but not finding one's wife. Misfortune from being trapped inside and out.

lines.textZh: 九四：来徐徐，困于金车，吝，有终。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fourth place: He comes very slowly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, but the end is reached.
lines.interpretationZh: 行动迟缓，困于华贵的车中。虽有困窘，但终能达成目标。
lines.interpretationEn: Coming slowly, oppressed in a golden carriage. Humiliation, but the goal is eventually reached.

lines.textZh: 九五：劓刖，困于赤绂，乃徐有说，利用祭祀。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: His nose and feet are cut off. Oppression at the hands of the man with the purple knee bands. Joy comes softly. It furthers one to make offerings and libations.
lines.interpretationZh: 遭受刑罚之困，被权贵所压迫。慢慢地喜悦到来，宜以祭祀求解。
lines.interpretationEn: Nose and feet cut off, oppressed by authority. Joy comes gradually. Offerings and devotion bring relief.

lines.textZh: 上六：困于葛藟，于臲卼，曰动悔有悔，征吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: He is oppressed by creeping vines. He moves uncertainly and says, 'Movement brings remorse.' If one feels remorse over this and makes a start, good fortune comes.
lines.interpretationZh: 被葛藤缠绕所困，摇摆不定。若能反省悔悟并付诸行动，则前往吉利。
lines.interpretationEn: Oppressed by entangling vines, wavering. If one repents and takes action, setting forth brings good fortune.

judgmentZh: 井：改邑不改井，无丧无得，往来井井。汔至，亦未繘井，羸其瓶，凶。
judgmentEn: The Well. The town may be changed, but the well cannot be changed. It neither decreases nor increases. They come and go and draw from the well. If one gets down almost to the water and the rope does not go all the way, or the jug breaks, it brings misfortune.
imageZh: 木上有水，井；君子以劳民劝相。
imageEn: Water over wood: the image of the Well. Thus the superior man encourages the people at their work, and exhorts them to help one another.
interpretationZh: 井卦象征水井，木上有水，汲水而上。井水不变，养育万民。君子效法井德，勤劳利民，互助互勉。
interpretationEn: Jing symbolizes the well. Water over wood draws nourishment upward. The well is constant, sustaining all. The superior man encourages mutual aid and diligent service.

lines.textZh: 初六：井泥不食，旧井无禽。
lines.textEn: Six at the beginning: One does not drink the mud of the well. No animals come to an old well.
lines.interpretationZh: 井底淤泥不可饮用，废弃的旧井连禽兽都不来。才德荒废则无人亲近。
lines.interpretationEn: The mud of the well is not drunk. No animals come to an old well. Neglected talent attracts no one.

lines.textZh: 九二：井谷射鲋，瓮敝漏。
lines.textEn: Nine in the second place: At the wellhole one shoots fishes. The jug is broken and leaks.
lines.interpretationZh: 井中只能射小鱼，水瓮破漏。才能未得善用，有才而无用武之地。
lines.interpretationEn: Shooting fish at the wellhole. The jug is broken and leaks. Talent is wasted without proper application.

lines.textZh: 九三：井渫不食，为我心恻，可用汲，王明，并受其福。
lines.textEn: Nine in the third place: The well is cleaned, but no one drinks from it. This is my heart's sorrow, for one might draw from it. If the king were clear-minded, good fortune might be enjoyed in common.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水已清却无人饮用，令人心痛。若遇明君赏识，则众人共享福泽。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is clean but unused. A sorrowful waste. If the king were wise, all could share its blessings.

lines.textZh: 六四：井甃，无咎。
lines.textEn: Six in the fourth place: The well is being lined. No blame.
lines.interpretationZh: 用砖石修砌井壁，无过失。修缮自身，打好基础。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is being lined with stone. No blame. Repair and strengthen the foundation.

lines.textZh: 九五：井冽，寒泉食。
lines.textEn: Nine in the fifth place: In the well there is a clear, cold spring from which one can drink.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水清冽，寒泉可饮。才德兼备，泽被众人。
lines.interpretationEn: A clear, cold spring in the well from which all may drink. Virtue and talent benefit everyone.

lines.textZh: 上六：井收勿幕，有孚元吉。
lines.textEn: Six at the top: One draws from the well without hindrance. It is dependable. Supreme good fortune.
lines.interpretationZh: 井水汲取无遮盖，人人可用。诚信可靠，大吉大利。
lines.interpretationEn: The well is open and dependable for all. With sincerity and reliability comes supreme good fortune.
